violations.text: At the time of inspection, the establishment did not have an on-site manager certified in food safety on duty as required at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Owner to ensure a person certified in food safety is on site at all times as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Meatballs held in steam hot hold unit were observed held at 106 to 157 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Meatballs were properly reheated to 170 degrees or higher at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Working container (Spray bottle) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Spray bottles to be properly labeled with the item contained. Labeled at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen area, is not tight-fitting (gap observed at the base). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The following equipment was observed to be in need of adjustment/repair: - The walk-in cooler door closing mechanism was observed to be lacking the "catch" portion of the closer - The gasket to the walk-in cooler door was observed not sealing properly - The hose connected from the mop sink faucet to the wall chemical dispenser unit was observed to be leaking Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The non-food-contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - The cookline equipment sides between equipment surfaces - The dough mixer Equipment is to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to preclude the buildup of soil and debris New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2026

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in need of repair/replacement: - Wallboard (FRP) observed with gaps and loosely attached in areas - Floor-wall juncture coving was observed peeling or missing in areas Physical Facilities are to be maintained in good repair New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting (Gap at the base of the door). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

At the time of inspection, the following items were observed to be in poor repair, in need of adjustment... - The Gaske to the prep unit behind the cookline prep units was observed to be torn - The bottom drawer to the cold drawer unit (Drawer not operating properly) Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was taken out of service for proper wash/rinse/sanitizing at the time of inspection. Ice was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

Per the PIC, chicken wings are partially cooked at 500 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 minutes prior to cooling and placing in the freezer unit (Temperature not monitored). Poultry must be cooked to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. At the time of inspection, chicken wings were placed back in the oven to finish fully cooking the wings to 165 Fahrenheit for 15 seconds as corrective action. PIC is to submit request for approval to RIDOH CFP Supervisor to perform partial cooking of chicken wings. PIC is to discontinue partial cooking of items until granted approval from RIDOH CFP Supervisor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

At the time of inspection, 2 packages of previously sliced deli salami were observed held beyond 7 days from preparation. Deli meats were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting (Gap at the base of the door). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

At the time of inspection, the following items were observed to be in poor repair, in need of adjustment... - The handle to the can opener (Screw observed to be broken) - The bottom drawer to the cold drawer unit (Drawer not operating properly) Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

The non-food-contact surfaces of the refrigeration/freezer equipment and cookline equipment were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Ice Machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit was taken out of service then washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Ice was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023
Oil skimmers were observed in poor repair (frayed). PIC elected to dispose of equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in the glass door cooler and the shelving holding equipment near the 3-door freezer in the kitchen has an accumulation of food residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023
